REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL
Network Infrastructure Upgrade — Riyadh HQ
Reference: RFP-2024-IT-0042
Issue Date: 1 March 2024
Closing Date: 31 March 2024

Issued by:
Saudi National Enterprises Company (SNEC)
Information Technology Department
King Fahd Road, Riyadh 12271
Kingdom of Saudi Arabia

================================================================================
SECTION 1 — EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
================================================================================

Saudi National Enterprises Company (SNEC) invites qualified Systems Integrators
to submit technical and commercial proposals for the complete overhaul of the
network infrastructure at our Riyadh Headquarters campus. The current network
was deployed in 2015 and can no longer support the organisation's growth
trajectory, cloud adoption strategy, or mandatory compliance obligations under
the National Cybersecurity Authority's Essential Cybersecurity Controls (NCA ECC
version 2.0).

The scope covers core switching and routing, perimeter security, wireless access,
and unified network management. The selected vendor must demonstrate prior
experience delivering comparable projects in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and hold
a valid Cisco Gold or Premier Certification at the time of contract award.

This RFP is structured in five sections. Vendors are required to respond to every
mandatory requirement. Partial responses will be disqualified.

================================================================================
SECTION 2 — SCOPE OF WORK
================================================================================

2.1 Project Overview

The Vendor shall supply, install, configure, test, and commission a complete
network infrastructure solution for the SNEC Riyadh HQ campus. The campus
comprises three buildings (Tower A, Tower B, and the Data Centre Annex) with
an aggregate floor area of approximately 18,500 square metres and an expected
user population of 1,200 staff upon full occupancy.

2.2 Deliverables

The Vendor shall deliver the following:

(a) Perimeter Security Layer
    Supply and install Next-Generation Firewall appliances at the campus
    perimeter and at the boundary of the Data Centre Annex. All firewalls
    must support IPS, application visibility, and URL filtering as baseline
    licensed features.

(b) Core and Distribution Switching
    Supply and install modular or fixed-configuration Layer 3 switches
    to form the core and distribution tiers. All switches must support
    IEEE 802.3bt PoE++ on access-layer ports and must provide 10G or higher
    uplink capacity between tiers.

(c) WAN and Branch Routing
    Supply and install integrated services routers at the campus WAN edge
    to terminate MPLS and Internet circuits. Routers must support SD-WAN
    capability via software licence and must include dual WAN interfaces
    as standard.

(d) Wireless Infrastructure
    Supply and install enterprise-class Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) access points
    throughout all buildings. The wireless solution must support centralised
    management, rogue AP detection, and seamless roaming across all floors.

(e) Network Management Platform
    Supply a perpetual or subscription licence for a unified network
    management platform capable of managing all devices in scope. The
    platform must provide topology discovery, configuration compliance
    checking, and integration with the client's existing SIEM.

2.3 Out of Scope

The following items are explicitly excluded from this RFP:
- Structured cabling and passive infrastructure
- UPS and power distribution
- Rack and cabinet procurement
- End-user devices (PCs, laptops, IP phones)

================================================================================
SECTION 3 — TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
================================================================================

3.1 Mandatory Requirements

The following requirements are mandatory. Non-compliance with any mandatory
requirement will result in the proposal being deemed non-responsive.

REQ-001 [MANDATORY]
The Vendor shall supply a minimum of ten (10) Cisco ASA 5516-X firewalls
with FirePOWER Services. Each unit must include a valid Threat Defence licence
covering IPS, AMP, and URL Filtering for a minimum period of three (3) years.
All firewalls must be configured in active/standby HA pairs.

REQ-002 [MANDATORY]
The Vendor shall supply a minimum of twenty (20) Cisco Catalyst 9300 Series
48-port PoE+ switches. Each switch must be configured with at least two (2)
10G SFP+ uplink modules. Stacking capability must be enabled on all units.
The switches must support IEEE 802.1X port-based authentication integrated
with the client's existing Microsoft Active Directory environment.

REQ-003 [MANDATORY]
The Vendor shall supply a minimum of five (5) Cisco 4331 Integrated Services
Routers. Each router must include a valid DNA Advantage licence and must support
IPsec VPN termination for a minimum of 500 concurrent tunnels. DMVPN Phase 3
support is required.

REQ-004 [MANDATORY]
The Vendor shall supply a minimum of fifty (50) Cisco Aironet 2800 Series
Wi-Fi 6 Access Points. APs must be zero-touch provisioned via Cisco DNA Centre
or Cisco Meraki Dashboard. The wireless solution must achieve a minimum RSSI of
-67 dBm at 95% of all occupied floor areas as validated by a pre-installation
RF survey report.

REQ-005 [MANDATORY]
The Vendor shall supply Cisco DNA Centre (physical or virtual appliance) as the
unified network management platform. The platform must be licensed to manage
all devices supplied under this contract for a minimum period of three (3) years.
Integration with Splunk SIEM via syslog and REST API is required.

REQ-006 [MANDATORY]
All supplied equipment must be new, unused, and sourced from Cisco-authorised
distribution channels. Grey market or refurbished equipment will result in
immediate disqualification. The Vendor must provide Cisco Smart Net Total Care
(SNTC) entitlement reports at the time of delivery.

REQ-007 [MANDATORY]
The Vendor must hold a valid Cisco Gold Certification or higher, issued to the
legal entity submitting this proposal, at the time of bid submission and
throughout the contract period.

3.2 Optional Requirements

The following requirements are desirable. Vendors are encouraged to propose
value-added elements; however, non-compliance will not disqualify a proposal.

REQ-008 [OPTIONAL]
The Vendor should provide a Software-Defined Access (SD-Access) fabric design
for the campus switching layer, enabling macro- and micro-segmentation without
VLAN proliferation.

REQ-009 [OPTIONAL]
The Vendor should include Cisco ThousandEyes endpoint monitoring licences for
up to 200 users to provide end-to-end visibility for SaaS and cloud-hosted
applications.

REQ-010 [OPTIONAL]
The Vendor should propose a managed services wrapper for NOC monitoring of all
devices in scope, with defined SLAs for incident response.

3.3 Conditional Requirements

REQ-011 [CONDITIONAL — applies if Vendor proposes SD-WAN]
If the Vendor proposes a Cisco SD-WAN (Viptela) solution at the WAN edge,
the solution must integrate with Cisco Umbrella for cloud-delivered DNS security
and must support application-aware routing policies for Microsoft 365 traffic.

================================================================================
SECTION 4 — COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
================================================================================

4.1 Regulatory Compliance

All solutions proposed under this RFP must comply with the following:

(a) NCA Essential Cybersecurity Controls (ECC) Version 2.0
    The firewall, switching, and network management components must satisfy
    controls under domains ECC-1 (Cybersecurity Governance) and ECC-2
    (Cybersecurity Defence). The Vendor must submit a compliance mapping
    document cross-referencing each proposed product against the relevant
    ECC control identifiers.

(b) ISO/IEC 27001:2022
    The Vendor's project delivery methodology must be consistent with the
    information security management principles defined in ISO/IEC 27001:2022.
    Evidence of the Vendor's own ISO 27001 certification is mandatory.

(c) SDAIA Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL)
    Where the network management platform processes or stores personal data
    of Saudi nationals, the Vendor must confirm compliance with the SDAIA PDPL.
    Data residency within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is required for all
    management-plane data.

4.2 Documentation Requirements

The Vendor must submit the following compliance documents with the proposal:

- High-level and detailed design documents
- Bill of Materials (BoM) with Cisco product SKUs and list prices
- NCA ECC-2 compliance mapping matrix
- ISO 27001 certificate (current, in-scope for SI services)
- Vendor's VAT registration certificate
- Proof of Cisco Gold or higher certification
- CVs of key project personnel (Project Manager, Lead Network Architect,
  Security Specialist)

================================================================================
SECTION 5 — COMMERCIAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS
================================================================================

5.1 Pricing

The Vendor shall submit a fully itemised commercial proposal in the provided
Bill of Quantities (BoQ) template. Prices must be stated in United States Dollars
(USD) exclusive of VAT. VAT at the applicable Saudi rate (15%) will be added to
the contract value.

5.2 Payment Terms

Payment shall be made according to the following schedule:
- 10% mobilisation advance upon contract signing
- 50% upon delivery and acceptance of all hardware at site
- 30% upon completion of installation and commissioning
- 10% upon final acceptance and sign-off

PAYMENT TERMS CLAUSE: All invoices must be settled within ninety (90) days of
the invoice date. The client reserves the right to offset any outstanding
penalties or deductions against amounts due to the Vendor. The Vendor expressly
waives any right to claim interest on late payments regardless of the cause
of delay.

5.3 Liability

LIABILITY CLAUSE: The Vendor accepts unlimited liability for any loss, damage,
or expense arising directly or indirectly from the performance or non-performance
of this contract, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data,
business interruption, and reputational damage. This liability shall not be
subject to any cap or limitation, financial or otherwise.

5.4 Performance Penalties

PENALTY CLAUSE: In the event of delay in delivery or commissioning beyond the
contractual milestone dates, the Vendor shall pay to the Client liquidated
damages at a rate of five percent (5%) of the total contract value per week
of delay, or part thereof. Liquidated damages shall accrue from the first day
of delay without notice and shall not be subject to any maximum cap. The Client
may deduct accrued liquidated damages from any amounts otherwise due to the Vendor.

5.5 Warranty

The Vendor must provide a minimum of three (3) years on-site warranty for all
hardware components, with next-business-day parts replacement and 8×5 on-site
engineer response. Optional extension to 24×7×4-hour response must be priced
separately.

5.6 Intellectual Property

All design documents, configurations, and work products produced under this
contract shall become the sole intellectual property of SNEC upon final payment.
The Vendor retains no licence to reuse designs or configurations for other clients.

5.7 Governing Law

This contract shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration under the Saudi Centre for Commercial
Arbitration (SCCA) rules, with arbitration seated in Riyadh.

================================================================================
END OF REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL
RFP-2024-IT-0042 | SNEC IT Department | Riyadh HQ Network Upgrade
================================================================================
